_meta.description: 5GC NF-specific attack patterns and vulnerability categories for directed code audit

common_patterns.name: Insufficient Input Validation on NAS/NGAP Messages
common_patterns.description: Binary protocol messages (NAS, NGAP, PFCP) parsed without proper length/bounds checking can lead to buffer overflows or panics
common_patterns.audit_focus: Check all Unmarshal/Decode calls for error handling and length validation before accessing decoded fields

common_patterns.name: Missing Authentication on SBI Endpoints
common_patterns.description: HTTP/2 SBI endpoints exposed without OAuth2 token verification or mutual TLS validation
common_patterns.audit_focus: Verify every SBI route group has middleware enforcing access token validation per TS 33.501 Section 13.3

common_patterns.name: Sensitive Data Exposure in Logs
common_patterns.description: SUPI, IMSI, authentication vectors, or encryption keys logged in plaintext
common_patterns.audit_focus: Search for logging statements that include SUPI, IMSI, SUCI, keys, or authentication vectors

common_patterns.name: Race Condition in UE Context Access
common_patterns.description: Concurrent access to UE context structures without proper synchronization
common_patterns.audit_focus: Check if UE context load/store operations use proper mutex locking, especially in NGAP/NAS handler goroutines

common_patterns.name: Insecure TLS Configuration
common_patterns.description: SBI endpoints using weak TLS versions, cipher suites, or disabled certificate verification
common_patterns.audit_focus: Verify TLS 1.2+ minimum, strong cipher suites, and no InsecureSkipVerify in production paths

common_patterns.name: Integer Overflow in Protocol Length Fields
common_patterns.description: Length fields in protocol messages used for memory allocation without overflow checks
common_patterns.audit_focus: Verify all length-based allocations from protocol messages are bounds-checked

common_patterns.name: Denial of Service via Resource Exhaustion
common_patterns.description: Unbounded goroutine spawning, channel fills, or memory allocation based on untrusted input
common_patterns.audit_focus: Check for rate limiting, connection limits, and bounded resource allocation in message handlers

AMF.name: NAS Security Mode Command Bypass
AMF.description: NAS messages accepted without completed security mode control procedure
AMF.audit_focus: Verify NAS message handlers check security context is established before processing protected messages

AMF.name: NGAP Message Injection via Malformed IE
AMF.description: NGAP protocol message with crafted Information Elements causing unexpected behavior
AMF.audit_focus: Check NGAP IE parsing for proper validation of mandatory/optional IEs and value ranges

AMF.name: SUCI Concealment Bypass
AMF.description: SUPI derivation from SUCI without proper null-scheme detection or protection scheme validation
AMF.audit_focus: Verify SUCI-to-SUPI resolution enforces encryption scheme and rejects null-scheme in production

AMF.name: Unauthorized Inter-AMF Handover
AMF.description: UE context transfer between AMFs without proper source AMF authentication
AMF.audit_focus: Verify source AMF identity is validated during UE context transfer

AMF.name: Registration Request Replay
AMF.description: Registration requests accepted without checking freshness (e.g., via uplink NAS count)
AMF.audit_focus: Check if NAS uplink count or anti-replay mechanisms are enforced on registration

SMF.name: Unauthorized PDU Session Establishment
SMF.description: PDU session creation without proper subscription/authorization checks
SMF.audit_focus: Verify DNN/S-NSSAI authorization checks against subscription data before session establishment

SMF.name: PFCP Session Hijacking
SMF.description: PFCP association or session modification messages accepted from unauthorized sources
SMF.audit_focus: Check PFCP message source validation and session ownership verification

SMF.name: GTP Tunnel Endpoint Manipulation
SMF.description: TEID assignment or F-TEID manipulation allowing traffic redirection
SMF.audit_focus: Verify TEID uniqueness and validate F-TEID references during PDR/FAR creation

SMF.name: QoS Policy Bypass
SMF.description: QoS flow rules that can be manipulated to bypass bandwidth/priority controls
SMF.audit_focus: Verify QoS parameters are validated against policy decisions from PCF

UPF.name: GTP-U Packet Injection
UPF.description: GTP-U data plane packets accepted without proper tunnel endpoint validation
UPF.audit_focus: Verify GTP-U packet TEID matches established PDR rules and source IP validation

UPF.name: PFCP Rule Manipulation
UPF.description: PDR/FAR/QER rules created or modified without proper SMF authorization
UPF.audit_focus: Verify PFCP association state and session ownership before accepting rule modifications

UPF.name: Data Plane Traffic Leak via Incorrect FAR
UPF.description: Forwarding Action Rules misconfigured to redirect user traffic to attacker-controlled endpoints
UPF.audit_focus: Audit FAR destination validation logic and redirect information handling

UPF.name: Kernel/eBPF Bypass in Fast Path
UPF.description: UPF fast path implementations using kernel bypass may skip security controls
UPF.audit_focus: Verify that kernel-bypass fast paths enforce the same PDR/FAR rules as the slow path

AUSF.name: 5G-AKA Authentication Vector Leak
AUSF.description: Authentication vectors (RAND, AUTN, XRES*) exposed or cached insecurely
AUSF.audit_focus: Verify AV storage is memory-only with proper cleanup and no logging of sensitive fields

AUSF.name: EAP-AKA' State Machine Bypass
AUSF.description: EAP-AKA' authentication state machine accepts out-of-sequence messages
AUSF.audit_focus: Verify strict state machine transitions and rejection of unexpected EAP message types

AUSF.name: SQN Synchronization Failure Exploitation
AUSF.description: Sequence number resynchronization mechanism exploitable for DoS or replay
AUSF.audit_focus: Check SQN resync rate limiting and validation of AUTS parameters

NRF.name: NF Profile Spoofing
NRF.description: Unauthorized NF registration or profile update allowing service impersonation
NRF.audit_focus: Verify NF registration validates identity claims against TLS client certificate

NRF.name: Service Discovery Information Leak
NRF.description: NF discovery endpoint returns excessive information about network topology
NRF.audit_focus: Check if discovery responses are filtered based on requester authorization scope

NRF.name: OAuth2 Token Scope Bypass
NRF.description: Access tokens issued with overly broad scope or without proper client authentication
NRF.audit_focus: Verify token scope validation and NF type-based access control

UDM.name: Subscriber Data Unauthorized Access
UDM.description: Subscription data retrieved or modified without proper authorization checks
UDM.audit_focus: Verify requester NF authorization before returning or modifying subscriber data

UDM.name: Authentication Credential Exposure
UDM.description: Permanent keys (K) or derived keys exposed through UDM interfaces
UDM.audit_focus: Verify authentication data generation keeps permanent keys internal and only exposes derived vectors

PCF.name: Policy Rule Injection
PCF.description: Unauthorized creation or modification of SM policies allowing QoS abuse
PCF.audit_focus: Verify policy decisions are based on authenticated subscriber context and proper authorization

PCF.name: Charging Policy Bypass
PCF.description: Manipulation of charging rules to avoid billing for data usage
PCF.audit_focus: Verify charging rules cannot be modified or bypassed through policy update requests
